title: Flood Immediate Safety
content: Move to higher ground. Avoid flood water because it can hide debris and electrical risks. Use safe drinking water from sealed sources.

title: Cyclone Shelter Protocol
content: Stay indoors in a reinforced room away from windows. Keep emergency kit, battery radio, medicines, and identification ready.

title: Earthquake Aftershock Advice
content: After shaking stops, evacuate unsafe structures, check for injuries, and prepare for aftershocks. Avoid open flames until gas leaks are ruled out.

title: Heatwave Vulnerable Group Care
content: Hydrate frequently, prioritize elderly and children, reduce outdoor activity at peak heat, and identify signs of heat exhaustion early.

title: Wildfire Smoke Protection
content: Limit outdoor exposure, use masks if available, close windows, and monitor respiratory symptoms. Evacuate early when official alerts escalate.

title: General Emergency Comms
content: When communication is unstable, use short structured messages: location, people count, injuries, urgent needs, and accessibility constraints.
